metadata.name: Ever Given Suez Canal Blockage
metadata.source: Suez Canal Authority / Lloyd's List / Allianz
metadata.description: The 400m container ship Ever Given ran aground in the Suez Canal, completely blocking one of the world's most critical maritime chokepoints for 6 days. Approximately 12% of global trade flows through the canal. The blockage held up an estimated $9.6B per day in trade, delayed over 400 vessels, and caused weeks of downstream port congestion even after the canal reopened.

recovery_milestones.description: Initial refloating attempts fail; tug boats deployed
Dredging operations intensify; some vessels begin rerouting via Cape of Good Hope
Ever Given refloated, canal traffic resumes

lessons_learned: A single chokepoint failure can cascade globally within hours; 12% of world trade funnels through the 193km Suez Canal.
Short-duration, high-impact events create disproportionate downstream congestion that outlasts the primary disruption by weeks.
Container shipping rates spiked and remained elevated for months, demonstrating the inelasticity of maritime logistics capacity.
Companies with diversified routing (Cape of Good Hope pre-arrangements) absorbed the shock significantly better.
Real-time vessel tracking (AIS data) proved critical for rapid situational awareness and rerouting decisions.
